title: InventoryGraph
description: The dependency graph of the legacy app. Node and edge KINDS are a neutral vocabulary that covers any component framework; the exact framework construct (Angular2+ NgModule, React hook, Vue composable, etc.) and its rich metadata live in each node's `sourceAdapter`. This keeps all framework-specific node kinds, construct metadata, DI styles, and edge kinds out of the core.

nodeId.description: Neutral, opaque id, e.g. 'module:app.invoices | component:invoiceTable | service:InvoiceService | template:invoices/list.html | route:/invoices | external:jquery.floatThead'.

kind.description: Neutral node classification. Mapping examples — Angular2+ component→'component', directive→'presentation' or 'behavior', pipe→'transform', injectable→'service', NgModule→'module'. React component→'component', hook→'behavior', context provider→'store'.

dependencyInjection.description: NEUTRAL DI summary. The framework-specific DI style (Angular2+ constructor tokens/inject(), React context/hooks) lives in sourceAdapter.

sourceAdapter.description: Framework-specific node metadata. For Angular2+ it carries selector/standalone/inputs/outputs/signals/providers (see adapters/angular2plus.schema.json).

kind.description: Neutral edge classification. Mapping — Angular2+ 'di-inject'→'depends-on'; 'route-loads'→'routes-to'; 'template-uses-component'→'renders'; 'content-projection'→'projects-into'; 'output-binding'→'emits'/'listens'; 'pipe-in-template'→'transforms-in'; external-library use→'uses-external'; 'module-imports'→'module-depends'.

sourceAdapter.description: Optional framework-specific edge detail (e.g. Angular2+ content-projection selector, DI token name).

unaccounted.description: MUST be empty before planning exit.
